channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: US strikes in Iran kill one, injure four; oil prices surge
item.description: The US military conducted fresh strikes in Iran's Khuzestan province, killing one person and injuring four others. The attacks targeted an agricultural water pumping station, according to Anadolu Agen...

item.title: LRT2 Now Accepts Visa and Mastercard Payments for Smoother Commuting
item.description: Commuters in the Philippines can now use contactless payments via Visa or Mastercard to pay for their Light Rail Transit Line 2 (LRT2) rides. This update, announced by Visa and Mastercard on July 13, ...

item.title: South African Man Smuggled 17.5kg Heroin Hidden in Coffee and Tea Bags, Arrested in Thailand
item.description: Thailand's Customs Department has arrested a South African man for attempting to smuggle 17.5 kilograms of heroin hidden in coffee and green tea bags. The arrest occurred on July 9, 2026, when officia...

item.title: Diverse Perspectives on Combating Terrorism and Extremism in the Region
item.description: Stakeholders have engaged in discussions highlighting varied approaches to addressing terrorism and extremism, emphasizing the need for multifaceted solutions. Security experts, community leaders, rel...

item.title: Trump's Election Legal Battles Continue Amid Judicial Pushback
item.description: President Donald Trump has faced ongoing legal challenges as his efforts to reshape election laws, including expanding voter roll verification and restricting mail-in voting, have been blocked by cour...

item.title: Deep-Sea Microbes Benefit from Pressure-Induced Nutrient Release
item.description: Scientists from the University of Southern Denmark discovered that extreme deep-sea pressure forces nutrients out of sinking organic particles, creating an unexpected food source for ocean microbes. T...

item.title: Peet Viljoen faces 400 charges in legal battle over bail
item.description: Former lawyer and reality TV star Peet Viljoen is embroiled in a complex legal battle as he seeks bail amid 400 charges linked to a R27.6 million property fraud scheme. The prosecution has submitted o...

item.title: Eastern Africa Power Pool Ministers Commit to Regional Electricity Market to Boost Energy Security and Economic Growth
item.description: Eastern Africa Power Pool (EAPP) member states have agreed to speed up the implementation of a regional electricity market aimed at expanding cross-border electricity trade and strengthening energy se...

item.title: Ambush of Church Leaders Deepened Naga-Kuki Violence and Expanded Blockades Across Manipur Hills
item.description: The article examines how the killing of three church leaders on May 13 became a major turning point in the ongoing Naga-Kuki conflict in Manipur. The pastors, including Reverend Vumthang Sitlhou, had ...

item.title: NZ’s regional air routes face challenges; government intervention needed for sustainable connectivity
item.description: Regional air routes in New Zealand are facing significant challenges due to rising fuel costs, economic pressures, and the 'thin-route' problem where flights are unprofitable. Air New Zealand has cut ...

item.title: Jammu and Kashmir Publishers Arrested Over Pro-Separatist Books
item.description: The Jammu and Kashmir Police's counter-intelligence unit has arrested three publishers, including Inderpaul Singh from Oberoi Book Service and Amardeep Singh & Girish Arora from Dominant Publishers in...

item.title: Belarusian Diaspora Members Face Retaliation from Minsk for Criticizing Lukashenko Regime
item.description: A report by the Digital Forensic Research Lab (DFRLab) reveals Belarusian diaspora members in Alberta, Canada, are facing retaliation from Minsk for their activism against authoritarian leader Alexand...

item.title: Tanzania Government Warns Against Unlawful Protests
item.description: The Tanzanian government has issued a clear directive to citizens planning demonstrations, emphasizing the need to adhere strictly to legal frameworks. Ambassador Sefue, representing the government, h...

item.title: INTERPOL's Global Anti-Fraud Operation Seizes Assets, Arrests Thousands
item.description: INTERPOL's Operation First Light 2026, which ran from January to April 2026, successfully dismantled a vast network of scammers across 97 countries. The operation led to the arrest of 5,811 suspects, ...

item.title: Pope Leo XIV Appoints Father Vincent Mpwaji as New Auxiliary Bishop for Dar es Salaam Archdiocese
item.description: In a good development for the Catholic Church in Tanzania, Pope Leo XIV has appointed Father Vincent Lawrence Mpwaji as the new Auxiliary Bishop of the Dar es Salaam Archdiocese. This appointment, ann...

item.title: Comparing Vienna's social housing model with Australia's challenges
item.description: The article explores the stark contrast between Vienna's efficient social housing system, which provides affordable homes to half its residents, and Australia's struggling housing market. An Australia...

item.title: Rare Color Photographs Reveal 1939 Golden Gate International Exposition's Art Deco Splendor
item.description: The article highlights a collection of rare color slides capturing the 1939 Golden Gate International Exposition, a pivotal world's fair held on Treasure Island in San Francisco Bay. Celebrating the c...

item.title: KMTC Student Anne Adhiambo's Parents Reveal Details of Her Brutal Murder
item.description: Anne Adhiambo, a final-year student at Kenyan Medical Training College (KMTC), was brutally murdered by five of her classmates over a minor dispute involving trousers. Her parents, who had come to the...

item.title: PSX declines 2,000 points as market sentiment remains weak
item.description: The Pakistan Stock Exchange (PSX) opened the week in the red, with the benchmark KSE-100 index plummeting 2,215.27 points to 180,026.5 from a previous close of 182,241.77. This sharp decline followed ...

item.title: Tech Billionaires and Crypto's Influence on Democracy
item.description: This documentary critiques the growing influence of tech billionaires and cryptocurrency magnates on democratic systems, highlighting how wealth concentration undermines traditional political structur...
